titleStmt.title: Letter from Thomas J. O'Connell to Matthew Nathan, 7 April 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Thomas J. O'Connell

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Thomas J. O'Connell, secretary of the Tralee and Dingle Railway Company, acknowledging the receipt of a letter sent amending the Tralee and Dingle (Committe and Management) Order (1896).Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939) as the under secretary for Ireland from 1914 to 1916. In 1916 Nathan offered his resignation following the outbreak of the Easter Rising.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/6211

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Tralee and Dingle Railway Company. Secretary's Office, Tralee 7th April, 1916. Sir:â I beg to acknowledge receipt of your 4399 â 6th inst.
enclosing draft of Order proposed to be made amenidng the Tralee
& Dingle (Committee of Management) Order 1896 which shall have
attention. I am, SIR,
Your obedient servant , T. J O'Connell
Secretary. The Under Secretary, Dublin Castle.
